title: croton suberosus
intro: Croton suberosus, also known as the leather leaf croton, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its unique, leathery leaves and vibrant green color. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. Consuming Croton suberosus has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Croton suberosus are made by boiling the leaves in water or infusing them in oil to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams that can be used to alleviate various health issues.
